title: LOADng seq_num wraps at 16 bits without coordinating with gradient table
description:
**Location:** python/src/lichen/loadng/discovery.py:85

**Bug:** The `originate_rreq()` method wraps `_own_seq` at 16 bits (`& 0xFFFF`). When seq_num wraps from 65535 to 0, existing gradient entries with higher seq_nums will be considered 'fresher' than new entries with seq_num near 0.

**Trigger:** Node sends 65536+ RREQs over its lifetime.

**Bad behavior:** After wrap, the gradient table's `_rank()` comparison will prefer old stale routes (seq_num 65000) over new routes (seq_num 100), because `65000 > 100`.

**Pattern:** Sequence number wrap-around needs either: (1) epoch coordination (like link-layer does), or (2) time-based expiration that ensures old entries are gone before wrap, or (3) RFC 1982 serial number arithmetic.
